# multierror

```go
import "github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
```

Package multierror provides an error type that can hold multiple errors.

## Constants

<a name="DefaultErrorsCapacity"></a>DefaultErrorsCapacity is the initial capacity for the errors slice.

```go
const DefaultErrorsCapacity = 12
```

<a name="Err"></a>
## type [Err](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L63-L70>)

Err is an error that can hold multiple errors.

```go
type Err struct {
    bruh.Err
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="Err.Add"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Add](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L234>)

```go
func (me *Err) Add(err ...error)
```

Add adds the given errors to the [Err](<#Err>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch")
	me.Add(nil, errors.New("e1"), nil, errors.New("e2"))
	fmt.Println(len(me.Errors()))

}
```

#### Output

```
2
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.AppendContext"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [AppendContext](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L351>)

```go
func (me *Err) AppendContext(context ctxerror.Context)
```

AppendContext implements \[ctxerror.contextAppender\] by combining the contexts of all contained errors. If multiple errors have the same context key, the value of the last error with that key is used.

<a name="Err.AppendTags"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [AppendTags](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L379>)

```go
func (me *Err) AppendTags(tags ctxerror.Tags)
```

AppendTags implements \[ctxerror.tagsAppender\] by combining the tags of all contained errors. If multiple errors have the same tag key, the value of the last error with that key is used.

<a name="Err.As"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [As](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L286>)

```go
func (me *Err) As(target any) bool
```

As implements [errors.As](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#As>) and attempts to map the target to any of the errors contained in the [Err](<#Err>). If any of the errors contained in the [Err](<#Err>) can be mapped to the target, true is returned and the target is set to the mapped error. Otherwise, false is returned and the target is not modified.

It respects the UnwrapBehavior option. If UnwrapFirst is set, the errors are checked in the order they were added. If UnwrapLast is set, the errors are checked in reverse order. If UnwrapNone is set, the target is not mapped to any of the contained errors and false is returned.

<a name="Err.Context"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Context](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L339>)

```go
func (me *Err) Context() map[string]map[string]any
```

Context implements \[ctxerror.contexter\] by combining the contexts of all contained errors. If multiple errors have the same context key, the value of the last error with that key is used.

<a name="Err.Error"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Error](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L122>)

```go
func (me *Err) Error() string
```



<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch")
	me.Add(errors.New("e1"))
	fmt.Println(me.Error())

}
```

#### Output

```
batch
  #0: e1
```

</p>

<a name="Err.ErrorOrNil"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [ErrorOrNil](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L188>)

```go
func (me *Err) ErrorOrNil() MultiErrorer
```

ErrorOrNil returns nil if the [Err](<#Err>) is nil or if it contains no errors. Otherwise it returns the [Err](<#Err>) itself.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	empty := multierror.New("batch")
	fmt.Println(empty.ErrorOrNil() == nil)

	nonEmpty := multierror.New("batch")
	nonEmpty.Add(errors.New("e1"))
	fmt.Println(nonEmpty.ErrorOrNil() != nil)

}
```

#### Output

```
true
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Errors"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Errors](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L118>)

```go
func (me *Err) Errors() []error
```

Errors returns the errors that are stored in the [Err](<#Err>). Might be nil if no errors have been added yet. Do not modify the returned slice.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch")
	me.Add(errors.New("e1"), errors.New("e2"))
	fmt.Println(len(me.Errors()))

}
```

#### Output

```
2
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Format"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Format](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L176>)

```go
func (me *Err) Format(s fmt.State, verb rune)
```

Format implements the fmt.Formatter interface. Use fmt.Sprintf\("%v", err\) to get a string representation of the error without an stack trace and fmt.Sprintf\("%\+v", err\) with a stack trace included.

<a name="Err.Grow"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Grow](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L102>)

```go
func (me *Err) Grow(capacity int)
```

Grow grows the internal slice of errors so it can accommodate at least the given number of errors without allocating.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch")
	me.Grow(4)
	fmt.Println(cap(me.Errors()) >= 4)

}
```

#### Output

```
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Is"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Is](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L315>)

```go
func (me *Err) Is(target error) bool
```

Is implements [errors.Is](<https://pkg.go.dev/errors/#Is>) and compares the target to any of the errors contained in the [Err](<#Err>). If any of the errors contained in the [Err](<#Err>) matches the target, true is returned. Otherwise, false is returned.

It respects the UnwrapBehavior option. If UnwrapFirst is set, the errors are checked in the order they were added. If UnwrapLast is set, the errors are checked in reverse order. If UnwrapNone is set, the target is not compared to any of the contained errors and false is returned.

<a name="Err.IsNil"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [IsNil](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L229>)

```go
func (me *Err) IsNil() bool
```

IsNil returns true if the [Err](<#Err>) is nil or if it contains no errors.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	empty := multierror.New("batch")
	fmt.Println(empty.IsNil())

	empty.Add(errors.New("e1"))
	fmt.Println(empty.IsNil())

}
```

#### Output

```
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Merge"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Merge](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L254>)

```go
func (me *Err) Merge(errs ...MultiErrorer)
```

Merge merges the given multi errors into the current error.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	left := multierror.New("left")
	left.Add(errors.New("a"))

	right := multierror.New("right")
	right.Add(errors.New("b"), errors.New("c"))

	left.Merge(right)
	fmt.Println(len(left.Errors()))

}
```

#### Output

```
3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Message"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Message](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L169>)

```go
func (me *Err) Message() string
```

Message returns the single, unformatted message of this error.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch")
	me.Add(errors.New("e1"))
	fmt.Println(me.Message())

}
```

#### Output

```
batch

```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.SingleOrNil"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [SingleOrNil](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L199>)

```go
func (me *Err) SingleOrNil() error
```

SingleOrNil is similar to \[ErrorOrNil\] but returns the single error if the [Err](<#Err>) contains exactly one error. If the [Err](<#Err>) is nil or contains no errors, nil is returned. If it contains more than one error, the [Err](<#Err>) itself is returned.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	empty := multierror.New("batch")
	fmt.Println(empty.SingleOrNil() == nil)

	one := multierror.New("batch")
	one.Add(errors.New("e1"))
	fmt.Println(one.SingleOrNil())

	many := multierror.New("batch")
	many.Add(errors.New("e1"), errors.New("e2"))
	fmt.Println(many.SingleOrNil() == many)

}
```

#### Output

```
true
e1
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Err.Tags"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Tags](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L367>)

```go
func (me *Err) Tags() map[string]string
```

Tags implements \[ctxerror.contexter\] by combining the tags of all contained errors. If multiple errors have the same tag key, the value of the last error with that key is used.

<a name="Err.Unwrap"></a>
### func \(\*Err\) [Unwrap](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L212>)

```go
func (me *Err) Unwrap() error
```

Unwrap returns nil if the [Err](<#Err>) is nil or if it contains no errors. If it contains exactly one error, that error is returned. If it contains more than one error, a chain of the errors is returned.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch", multierror.Options{UnwrapBehavior: multierror.UnwrapFirst})
	me.Add(errors.New("e1"), errors.New("e2"))
	fmt.Println(me.Unwrap())

}
```

#### Output

```
e1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FilterFunc"></a>
## type [FilterFunc](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L32>)

FilterFunc is a predicate used to filter errors.

```go
type FilterFunc func(error) bool
```

<a name="MultiErrorer"></a>
## type [MultiErrorer](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L37-L53>)

MultiErrorer is an interface that is implemented by the [Err](<#Err>) type. It is used to distinguish between errors created by this package and other errors. It is not intended to be used by users of this package.

```go
type MultiErrorer interface {
    Message() string

    // Error collection access / mutation
    Errors() []error
    Grow(capacity int)
    Add(err ...error)
    Merge(err ...MultiErrorer)
    Unwrap() error

    // Nil/return helpers
    IsNil() bool
    ErrorOrNil() MultiErrorer
    SingleOrNil() error
    // contains filtered or unexported methods
}
```

<a name="Errorf"></a>
### func [Errorf](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L90>)

```go
func Errorf(options Options, format string, args ...any) MultiErrorer
```

Errorf creates a new [Err](<#Err>) with a formatted message.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.Errorf(multierror.Options{}, "batch %d", 42)
	fmt.Println(me.IsNil())

}
```

#### Output

```
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="New"></a>
### func [New](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L74>)

```go
func New(msg string, options ...Options) MultiErrorer
```

New creates a new [Err](<#Err>) with the given message and options. You can add more errors later by calling \[Add\].

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch import")
	fmt.Println(me.IsNil())

}
```

#### Output

```
true
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Options"></a>
## type [Options](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L56-L60>)

Options configures behavior of an [Err](<#Err>) created by [New](<#New>) or [Errorf](<#Errorf>).

```go
type Options struct {
    UnwrapBehavior UnwrapBehavior
    LimitPrint     int
    Filter         FilterFunc
}
```

<details><summary>Example (Filter)</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch", multierror.Options{Filter: func(err error) bool {
		return err.Error() != "skip"
	}})
	me.Add(errors.New("keep"), errors.New("skip"), errors.New("keep2"))
	fmt.Println(len(me.Errors()))

}
```

#### Output

```
2
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Limit Print)</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch", multierror.Options{LimitPrint: 2})
	me.Add(
		errors.New("error1"),
		errors.New("error2"),
		errors.New("error3"),
		errors.New("error4"),
		errors.New("error5"),
	)
	fmt.Println(me.Error())

}
```

#### Output

```
batch
  #0: error1
  #1: error2
  ... and 3 more errors
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Unwrap Behavior)</summary>
<p>



```go
package main

import (
	"errors"
	"fmt"

	"github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/pkg/multierror"
)

func main() {
	me := multierror.New("batch", multierror.Options{UnwrapBehavior: multierror.UnwrapLast})
	me.Add(errors.New("first"), errors.New("last"))
	fmt.Println(me.Unwrap())

}
```

#### Output

```
last
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="UnwrapBehavior"></a>
## type [UnwrapBehavior](<https://github.com/aisbergg/go-bruh/blob/main/pkg/multierror/multi_error.go#L17>)

UnwrapBehavior controls how multiple wrapped errors are unwrapped.

```go
type UnwrapBehavior int
```

<a name="UnwrapFirst"></a>

```go
const (
    // UnwrapFirst returns only the first wrapped error when unwrapping.
    UnwrapFirst UnwrapBehavior = iota
    // UnwrapLast returns only the last wrapped error when unwrapping.
    UnwrapLast
    // UnwrapNone stops unwrapping at the multi error.
    UnwrapNone
)
```
